propositions: Publication is gated only by a v*-tag push check that never verifies the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary pushed tag to publish.
Switch the release trigger to workflow_dispatch run from main so release source is reviewed-by-construction, and remove the push:tags publish trigger.
Enforce version lockstep before build by asserting the dispatch version input equals pyproject.toml, server.json, and each server.json.packages[].version, failing on mismatch.
Build and publish directly via PyPA Trusted Publishing OIDC with no intermediate tag relay or PAT, creating the v* tag and GitHub Release only after a successful publish.
Add a fail-closed release-time preflight querying the pypi environment config that aborts if required reviewers are missing or the deployment-branch policy is not main-only.
Extract version-lockstep, ancestry, and env-preflight guards into unit-testable helpers and demonstrate the guards fire on a real dispatch run with invalid inputs before any publish.
